title: Browser compatibility pass for public samples
body: Run the basic, media/BinData, and diagnostic HWPX samples on Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari. Record what opens, what exports, and any console errors. This proves the public demo works across real user browsers.

title: Add malformed XML HWPX fixture
body: Add a synthetic HWPX fixture with invalid section XML and expected package doctor output. The fixture should prove that XML parse failures are blocked from unsafe source-preserving export.

title: Add section XML diff view after HWPX export
body: Show a before/after diff for changed section XML after HWPX export. The goal is to make source-preserving edits auditable for schools, agencies, courts, and companies.

title: Add relationship cleanup repair rule
body: Expand safe repair beyond missing root mimetype. Start with a manifest or relationship entry that points to a missing target, then produce a before/after report proving original entries are preserved.

title: Add media thumbnail preview in Package Explorer
body: For safe image-like media entries, show a small preview or metadata summary in Package Explorer. Keep the workflow local-first and make unsupported formats explicit.

title: Add headers, footers, and footnotes fixture
body: Create a synthetic HWPX fixture with header/footer/footnote-like structures and expected doctor warnings. This turns common public-sector layout features into tested compatibility claims.

title: Add merged-table fixture and geometry warning
body: Create a synthetic table fixture with merged-cell-like structure. Verify text extraction/export and make geometry limitations visible in the compatibility report.

title: Pin CDN dependencies as local vendor assets
body: Replace CDN runtime dependencies with pinned local assets or an audited build step. This is important for offline use and sensitive document workflows.

title: Add PWA offline install path
body: Make the static app installable and usable offline after first load. Keep all document processing local and document the browser support level.

title: Add CLI validator using the same fixture contracts
body: Expose package doctor and fixture validation as a CLI flow so developers and institutions can batch-check HWPX files outside the browser UI.
